prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, low-contrast frame. Large text appears to be present across the screen, but it is too dim and blurred to read reliably. No people or products are clearly visible in this opening frame.

By around 60 seconds, the image changes to a clearer product display. A box of **Jell-O Instant Pudding** is shown centered slightly lower than mid-frame against a plain light-gray background. The box is angled slightly, giving it a three-dimensional appearance. The front reads **“JELL-O”** in large letters and **“INSTANT PUDDING”** beneath it. A dark label or badge on the lower-left front of the box includes the word **“New.”** Above and around the box, large white handwritten-style words appear: **“GOOD GOOD”** on the upper left and **“BUSY-DAY DESSERT”** on the upper right. The image has a monochrome, vintage television look with soft focus and visible video noise.

In the final frame, the same product shot remains on screen, now slightly sharper and more centered. The text **“GOOD GOOD BUSY-DAY DESSERT”** is clearly visible above the package. The Jell-O box remains the main visual focus, with **“JELL-O,” “INSTANT PUDDING,”** and **“New”** legible on the front. Small fine print appears along the bottom edge of the frame, but it is too blurred to read clearly. No characters, actions, or additional settings are visible in the sampled frames.
